title: A letter my great-aunt never sent
summary: Tell the story of an old, unsent letter found in the family, and what it reveals about a person and a choice.
topic: An old letter that was written but never sent
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences telling the story of a letter that someone wrote but never sent. Describe how you found it, what it said, and why you think it was never sent. Use past simple, past perfect, and the connectors 'because' and 'after'.
hintStarters: We found the letter after my great-aunt died, folded inside…
It was written to a man none of us had ever heard about, and…
I think she never sent it because…
Reading it, I felt that I knew her a little better, even though…
sampleText: After my great-aunt Nadia died, we cleared her flat in Odesa and found a letter at the back of a drawer. It was inside an envelope with a stamp on it, but the stamp had never been used. The letter was forty years old. She had written it to a man named Yuri, and none of us had ever heard his name before. In the letter she said she was sorry, and that she had been wrong to leave, and that she still thought about a certain summer by the sea. The handwriting was steady at the start and shaky near the end. We do not know who Yuri was, and we never will, because everyone who might have known is gone. I think she never sent it because she was afraid. Maybe she wrote it to feel better and then changed her mind. Maybe she meant to send it and waited one day too long. After we read it, we sat in silence for a while. My mother said she had always thought Nadia was a hard, closed woman who never loved anyone. The letter said something different. We did not send it, of course. There was no address, and the man was probably gone too. But I keep thinking about the stamp she bought and never used.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past perfect for events before the main past story
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Câu 'She had written it to a man named Yuri' và 'the stamp had never been used' dùng quá khứ hoàn thành để chỉ những việc xảy ra trước thời điểm chính trong quá khứ (lúc tìm thấy lá thư). Quá khứ đơn dùng cho câu chuyện chính, quá khứ hoàn thành lùi về trước nó.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: The sentences 'She had written it to a man named Yuri' and 'the stamp had never been used' use the past perfect to mark things that happened before the main past moment (finding the letter). Past simple carries the main story, and past perfect steps back before it.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'After' to show order of events
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Câu 'After we read it, we sat in silence for a while' dùng 'after' để cho thấy việc đọc xảy ra trước việc ngồi im lặng. 'After' giúp người đọc biết thứ tự các hành động.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: The sentence 'After we read it, we sat in silence for a while' uses 'after' to show that the reading happened before the sitting in silence. 'After' helps the reader know the order of the actions.

sampleTranslationVi: Sau khi bà cô Nadia của tôi qua đời, chúng tôi dọn dẹp căn hộ của bà ở Odesa và tìm thấy một lá thư ở phía sau một ngăn kéo. Nó nằm trong một phong bì có dán tem, nhưng con tem chưa bao giờ được dùng. Lá thư đã bốn mươi năm tuổi. Bà đã viết nó cho một người đàn ông tên Yuri, và không ai trong chúng tôi từng nghe tên ông trước đó. Trong thư bà nói bà xin lỗi, rằng bà đã sai khi ra đi, và rằng bà vẫn nghĩ về một mùa hè nào đó bên biển. Nét chữ vững ở đầu và run rẩy về cuối. Chúng tôi không biết Yuri là ai, và sẽ không bao giờ biết, vì mọi người có thể biết đều đã mất. Tôi nghĩ bà không bao giờ gửi nó vì bà sợ. Có lẽ bà viết để thấy nhẹ lòng rồi đổi ý. Có lẽ bà định gửi nhưng chờ thêm một ngày quá lâu. Sau khi đọc nó, chúng tôi ngồi im lặng một lúc. Mẹ tôi nói bà luôn nghĩ Nadia là một người phụ nữ cứng rắn, khép kín, chẳng yêu ai bao giờ. Lá thư nói điều khác. Tất nhiên chúng tôi không gửi nó. Không có địa chỉ, và người đàn ông đó có lẽ cũng đã mất. Nhưng tôi cứ nghĩ mãi về con tem bà mua mà không bao giờ dùng.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the best-written sentence about finding the letter.
options: We found a letter at the back of a drawers.
We finded a letter at the back of a drawer.
We found a letter in the back from a drawer.
We found a letter at the back of a drawer.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Found … at the back of a drawer' is correct. 'A drawers' mixes singular and plural; 'finded' is wrong ('found'); 'in the back from' uses wrong prepositions.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the past tense of 'find'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct passive past-perfect sentence about the stamp.
options: The stamp had never been used.
The stamp had never be used.
The stamp has never been use.
The stamp had never been use.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Had never been used' is the correct past-perfect passive. 'Had never be used' and 'had never been use' use wrong forms; 'has' is present perfect, not past.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'had been', what form of the main verb is needed?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past-perfect sentence about writing the letter.
options: She had wrote it to a man named Yuri.
She had written it to a man named Yuri.
She has written it to a man named Yuri before.
She had write it to a man named Yuri.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Had written' is correct. 'Had wrote' uses the wrong participle; 'has written … before' is present perfect; 'had write' uses the base verb.
mcQuestions.hint: The past participle of 'write' is 'wrote' or 'written'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Select the best sentence about nobody having heard the name.
options: None of us had ever hear his name before.
None of us have ever heard his name before then.
None of us had ever heard his name before.
None of us had never heard his name before.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Had ever heard' is correct. 'Had ever hear' uses the base verb; 'have … then' is the wrong tense pairing; 'had never … before' creates a double negative.
mcQuestions.hint: With 'none of us', do we use 'ever' or 'never' here to avoid a double negative?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence using 'because' to give a reason.
options: She never sent it because of she was afraid.
She never sent it, so she was afraid.
She never send it because she was afraid.
She never sent it because she was afraid.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Because + clause' is correct. 'Because of' needs a noun; 'so' shows result, not reason; 'send' should be past 'sent'.
mcQuestions.hint: Does 'because' or 'because of' come before a full clause?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the most natural sentence about changing her mind.
options: Maybe she wrote it to feel better and then changed her mind.
Maybe she wrote it for feel better and then changed her mind.
Maybe she wrote it to feel better and then changed her mind then.
Maybe she wrote it to feeling better and then changed her mind.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'To feel better' (purpose) and 'changed her mind' are correct. 'For feel' and 'to feeling' use wrong forms; option 3 adds a redundant 'then'.
mcQuestions.hint: To show purpose, do we use 'to feel' or 'for feel'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence using 'after' to order events.
options: After we read it, we sit in silence for a while.
After we read it, we sat in silence for a while.
After we reading it, we sat in silence for a while.
After we read it, we sat in silence since a while.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'After we read it' and 'sat' are correct. 'Sit' is present; 'we reading' is wrong; 'since a while' should be 'for a while'.
mcQuestions.hint: Do we say 'for a while' or 'since a while'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Select the best sentence about the mother's old opinion.
options: She had always think Nadia was a hard, closed woman.
She always had thought Nadia is a hard, closed woman.
She had always thought Nadia was a hard, closed woman.
She had always thinked Nadia was a hard, closed woman.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Had always thought' is correct. 'Had always think' uses the base verb; option 3 misplaces 'always' and uses present 'is'; 'thinked' is wrong.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the past participle of 'think'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural sentence about why they could not send it.
options: There was not address, and the man was probably gone too.
There was any address, and the man was probably gone too.
There were no address, and the man was probably gone too.
There was no address, and the man was probably gone too.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'There was no address' is correct. 'Not address' needs 'no'; 'any' is used in questions/negatives differently; 'there were' disagrees with the singular 'address'.
mcQuestions.hint: Before a singular noun in a statement, do we use 'no' or 'any'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the best closing sentence about the unused stamp.
options: I keep thinking about the stamp she bought and never used.
I keep thinking about the stamp she bought and never use.
I keep to think about the stamp she bought and never used.
I keep thinking about the stamp which she bought it and never used.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'The stamp she bought and never used' is correct, with 'keep thinking'. 'Never use' should be past; 'keep to think' uses the wrong form; option 4 adds an extra 'it'.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'keep', do we use 'thinking' or 'to think'?

criticalThinkingQuestion: If you found a private letter that changed how you saw someone in your family, would you want to know more, or would you rather leave the mystery alone? Why?
